title: Creamy Mac & Cheese
intro: Curtis Stone does comfort food beautifully. The Thermomix makes a lump-free cheese sauce in minutes — stir through cooked pasta and you've got the creamiest mac and cheese.
steps: Grate the cheddar 8 sec / 0°C / speed 8; set aside.
Add butter, flour and milk. Cook 8 min / 90°C / speed 4 until thickened to a smooth white sauce.
Add the cheese, mustard and nutmeg. Mix 20 sec / 0°C / speed 4 until melted and glossy. Season.
Stir the cooked macaroni through the sauce. For a crust, top with breadcrumbs and grill a few minutes; finish 1 min / 0°C / no speed.
tips: Grating the cheese in the Thermomix means it melts smooth, never grainy.
A spoon of mustard sharpens the whole thing.

faq.q: Why won't the cheese sauce go lumpy?
faq.a: Cooking the flour, butter and milk together at speed 4 keeps it constantly whisked and silky.

faq.q: Is this the original Curtis Stone recipe?
faq.a: No. This is Chef Aly’s independent Thermomix reinterpretation inspired by Curtis Stone and mac and cheese; it is not endorsed by or affiliated with the chef.

faq.q: Can I make this Creamy Mac & Cheese in a TM6 or TM7?
faq.a: Yes. The method is written with TM6 and TM7 settings, using metric weights, clear times, temperatures and speeds.

description: A Chef Aly Thermomix reinterpretation of Curtis Stone-style mac and cheese: metric ingredients, exact TM6 and TM7 times, temperatures and speeds for Australian home cooks.
